name: Laura Fine
bio: Laura Fine is an Illinois state senator representing the 9th district — the same seat once held by Daniel Biss. A former journalist, she is running for the open IL-09 congressional seat. She has raised approximately $2M. (Source: FEC H6IL09194; Wikipedia)

jacket.note: ~$2M raised through Dec 31, 2025 per FEC filings.
jacket.donors_note: Donors list is PARTIAL — only notable/flagged donors are itemized. total_raised ($2,000,000) reflects the full FEC aggregate total (H6IL09194). Full donor breakdown pending ETL import. The Gessner family entry represents $14,000 of documented AIPAC-network contributions; remaining $1,986,000 is unitemized in this record.

red_flags.label: AIPAC-Connected Donors Flooding Campaign
red_flags.detail: Evanston Now reported in October 2025 that AIPAC-linked donors are actively funding Fine's IL-09 campaign, including $14,000 from the Gessner family, documented AIPAC donors. WBEZ reported in February 2026 that pro-Israel groups and affiliates have poured $13.7M into Chicago-area primaries.

career_history.highlight: Senate Mental Health Committee; behavioral health policy leader

policy_platform.position: Lead advocate for expanded mental health funding in Illinois; authored mental health parity legislation.
Supports increased K-12 and higher education funding; backed Evidence-Based Funding formula.
Consistent votes for environmental protection legislation; supports clean energy transition.

social_pulse.summary: Low public profile outside her district. Positive among mental health advocacy community. No major controversies.

trust_indicators.label: Mental health policy champion in Illinois Senate
